hero.title: 3 Days in Ayodhya.
hero.titleAccent: The Birthplace of Lord Rama, Brought to Life.
hero.intro: Ayodhya has stepped back into the spotlight, and with a knowledgeable guide its layers of legend, history and devotion truly open up. The new Ram Mandir, the ancient ghats and the living mythology make this a moving short pilgrimage.

breadcrumb.label: 3 Days Explore Ayodhya with a Professional Guide

overview.heading: 3 Days Explore Ayodhya with a Professional Guide: The Full Picture
body: Ayodhya is one of the seven holiest cities in Hinduism, revered as the birthplace of Lord Rama. With the grand Ram Janmabhoomi temple now complete, the city has become a major pilgrimage destination once more, drawing devotees from across the world to its temples and the sacred Sarayu river.

This 3 day Ayodhya package from Malaysia comes with a dedicated professional guide who brings the city's mythology, history and traditions vividly to life. You visit the Ram Mandir, Hanuman Garhi, the serene Guptar Ghat and the key sites woven into the Ramayana, all at a peaceful pace.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Ram Janmabhoomi Temple
items.body: The magnificent new Ram Mandir, built on the believed birthplace of Lord Rama, is the spiritual heart of Ayodhya and one of the most significant temples in modern India.

items.title: Hanuman Garhi & the Temples
items.body: Climb to Hanuman Garhi, the hilltop temple guarding the city, and visit Kanak Bhawan and the many shrines that fill Ayodhya with devotion and colour.

items.title: Guptar Ghat & the Sarayu River
items.body: The peaceful Guptar Ghat, where legend says Lord Rama departed his earthly life, is the most serene spot in Ayodhya. The evening aarti on the Sarayu is deeply atmospheric.

days.title: Arrive Ayodhya
days.description: Met at Ayodhya or Lucknow airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening free, perhaps the Sarayu river aarti.

days.title: Full Day Ayodhya with Guide
days.description: A full day with your professional guide covering the city's sacred sites and their stories.

days.title: Final Darshan, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. A last darshan and prasad before your airport transfer.

whyBook.heading: Ayodhya, Explained by an Expert
whyBook.body: Ayodhya rewards travellers who understand its stories. Our dedicated professional guide makes the difference, turning temple visits into a living journey through the Ramayana. Dhesu has arranged spiritual India trips since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures

points.title: A Real Team to Call

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing

sections.heading: Ayodhya Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
Ayodhya has returned to the centre of India's spiritual map, and a guided trip is the best way to appreciate it. The new Ram Mandir is the obvious highlight, but the city's older temples, ghats and legends are just as rewarding with the right guide.

This private package includes a dedicated professional guide who brings the mythology and history alive, so your visit is meaningful rather than just a checklist.

Ayodhya combines naturally with Varanasi and Prayagraj for a deeper spiritual circuit.

sections.heading: Combining Ayodhya with Varanasi
sections.body: Many pilgrims pair Ayodhya with Varanasi, the two great spiritual cities of the north. Our 5 day Best of Varanasi plus Ayodhya package brings them together, and the 6 day Sacred Triangle adds Prayagraj for the full experience.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Ayodhya Airport or Lucknow Airport (AYJ / LKO). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Ayodhya?
items.answer: October to March offers the most comfortable weather. Ram Navami (around March-April) and Deepotsav around Diwali are spectacular but extremely crowded. Summers are very hot. Winter is ideal for temple visits and the river aarti.

items.question: Is a guide really necessary in Ayodhya?
items.answer: It makes a big difference. So much of Ayodhya's meaning lies in its connection to the Ramayana and its history. A professional guide turns the visit into a rich, contextual journey rather than a series of stops.

items.question: Can I combine Ayodhya with Varanasi or Prayagraj?
items.answer: Yes. These cities form a natural spiritual circuit. Our 5 day Varanasi plus Ayodhya and 6 day Sacred Triangle packages combine them, including the Triveni Sangam at Prayagraj.
